functions.name: readEasStamps
functions.description: Walk every contact card in the given address book and read EAS legacy property-bag stamps off the underlying nsIAbCard. Legacy EAS4 wrote `EAS-Spouse`, `EAS-Yomi*`, `Children` (and friends) via the older `setProperty()` interface; modern Thunderbird does not surface those properties in the WebExtension-visible vCard, so we have to reach for them via XPCOM. The contact migration in `modules/upgrades.mjs` uses the result to lift each legacy stamp into a corresponding `X-EAS-*` vCard property.

parameters.name: bookId
parameters.description: Address book UID (the same id the WebExtension addressBooks API exposes).
